pattern: The reachable ceiling at every position is bounded both by how far it can climb from its left neighbor and how far it can climb from its right neighbor, so one forward sweep and one backward sweep of step-limited relaxation converge on the tightest ceiling everywhere, and the answer is the tallest ceiling reached.
hints: Ignoring the extra caps for a moment, the biggest value position i could ever reach is bounded by how much the step limits allow it to grow away from the fixed starting value at position 0.

First compute that unconstrained forward ceiling from the start, then clamp the ceiling at each capped position down to its cap — but a cap doesn't only affect its own position, since neighbors can't jump further than the per-step limit either.

After applying the caps, run one left-to-right pass tightening each ceiling to at most the previous position's (already tightened) ceiling plus that step's limit, then run one right-to-left pass doing the mirror image using the following position's ceiling; the answer is the largest value left in the ceiling array.

edge_inputs.description: sample: two separated caps, best peak sits between them
sample: one cap near the middle, growth resumes strongly after it
smallest possible n with the single mandatory restriction on the only non-zero index
a very tight cap right after the start forces the whole sequence low despite generous diffs afterward
cap on the last index only — must propagate backward through the whole chain
cap on the first non-zero index only — must propagate forward through the whole chain
every index restricted to the same small cap, growth is impossible anywhere
restrictions listed out of index order in the input array

stress.description: moderate-size chain with several scattered restrictions and small diffs, exercising forward/backward propagation across many caps
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = size
    diff = [rng.randint(1, 10) for _ in range(n - 1)]
    idxs = list(range(1, n))
    rng.shuffle(idxs)
    num_restrictions = max(1, n // 20)
    chosen = sorted(idxs[:num_restrictions])
    restrictions = [[i, rng.randint(1, 1000000)] for i in chosen]
    return [n, restrictions, diff]
